_comment: Gene catalog. All entries are ABSTRACT, VIRTUAL simulator data inspired by Drosophila genetics. They are not real biological sequences and contain no real-world gene-editing or wet-lab instructions. 'position' is an abstract map coordinate used for ordering and (later) linkage.

genes.description: Controls deposition of eye pigment. Loss removes pigment, giving white eyes.
genes.educational_note: The classic first Drosophila mutant; X-linked recessive white eyes.

genes.description: Affects dark cuticle pigment. Loss yields a yellowish body.
genes.educational_note: X-linked; reduced melanin gives the body a yellow tone.

genes.description: Affects cuticle darkening. Loss produces a darker (ebony) body.
genes.educational_note: Autosomal recessive; opposite tendency to yellow.

genes.description: Required for normal wing growth. Loss gives small, vestigial wings.
genes.educational_note: Recessive reduced wings; usually viable but flight-impaired.

genes.description: Master regulator of eye formation. Loss reduces or removes eyes.
genes.educational_note: Strongly reduced eye size can mask eye-color genes (epistasis, later).

genes.description: Hox gene specifying body-segment identity in the thorax.
genes.educational_note: A dominant gain-of-function can transform antennae toward leg identity.

genes.description: Signaling gene needed for wing and pattern formation.
genes.educational_note: Strong disruption can override wing-shape genes (epistasis, later).

genes.description: Signaling gene; dosage-sensitive effects on wing margins and neural tissue.
genes.educational_note: Semi-dominant notched wings when one copy is reduced; full loss is more severe.

genes.description: Hox gene specifying posterior thoracic/abdominal identity.
genes.educational_note: Loss can transform segment identity; classic homeotic example.

genes.description: Anterior axis-patterning factor in early development.
genes.educational_note: Simplified here as a recessive developmental factor.

genes.description: Core circadian-clock gene influencing activity rhythm.
genes.educational_note: Variants shorten or lengthen the behavioral activity cycle.
Loss reduces a simplified learning/behavior score.

genes.description: Abstract polygenic body-size locus. Small additive effects from several such loci combine to set body size.
genes.educational_note: Body size is polygenic: no single gene controls it.

genes.description: Abstract polygenic body-size locus (see locus A).
genes.educational_note: Body size is polygenic: no single gene controls it.

genes.description: Abstract polygenic body-size locus (see locus A).
genes.educational_note: Body size is polygenic: no single gene controls it.

genes.description: Abstract modifier locus whose alleles suppress or enhance the severity of wing-defect genes such as vestigial.
genes.educational_note: Modifier genes do not cause a phenotype alone; they change how strongly another gene is expressed.
